output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 4/4.
Batch 1: Executed batch 1 task T1. `taskReferencesGeneration` is now exported and matches the added generation reference fields required for travel tasks. `git diff --check` passed; file-level ESLint still reports a pre-existing restricted import on `@/integrations/supabase/client` in the same file.
Batch 2: Executed batch 2 task T2. Added `useActiveTaskClips` with a single project-wide active-task query, client-side generation matching, realtime query invalidation, and exported optimistic clip activation state.
Batch 3: Executed batch 3 task T3. Wired `useActiveTaskClips` into `TimelineEditor` and added the clip-overlay spinner path in `ClipAction` with spinner priority over the stale badge.
Batch 4: Executed batch 4 task T4. Final validation now passes: `npx tsc --noEmit` succeeded, the four-file `eslint` command succeeded, and a one-off verification script confirmed the active-task clip data flow and was deleted afterward.

task_updates.executor_notes: Exported `taskReferencesGeneration` and extended it to match `params.input_image_generation_ids`, `params.start_image_generation_id`, `params.end_image_generation_id`, plus `orchestrator_details.input_image_generation_ids`. Verified those fields against resolver persistence in `travelBetweenImages.ts:188` and `individualTravelSegment.ts:334-335,360-361`. `git diff --check` passed; file-level ESLint still reports the pre-existing restricted-import violation already present in this file.

Created `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts` with one React Query fetch for all active tasks in the selected project (`useQuery` at lines 177-191) and client-side filtering across a registry-derived `generationAssetMap` (`useMemo` at lines 154-175 and 193-211) using imported `taskReferencesGeneration`. Added realtime invalidation for matching project task events via `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent` (`tasks-created`/`tasks-updated`, lines 221-242). Added exported optimistic support with an 8s timeout through module-level state and `addOptimisticActive` (`lines 24-123`), then unioned queried and optimistic asset keys (`lines 244-256`). Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts`, `git diff --check -- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` all passed.

`TimelineEditor` now reads `activeTaskAssetKeys` from `useActiveTaskClips` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:218`) and computes/passes `isTaskActive` inside `getActionRender` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:411-457`), with `activeTaskAssetKeys` added to the callback dependency list (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:459-480`). `ClipAction` now accepts `isTaskActive` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:18-48`), renders a spinner badge in the stale-badge position and only falls back to the stale badge when no task is active (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372`), and includes `isTaskActive` in `areClipActionPropsEqual` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:406-448`). Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx`, `git diff --check -- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` all passed.

Final verification passed after removing the remaining restricted static Supabase facade import from `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingGenerationTasks.ts` (`lines 94-105` now use a dynamic import inside the query function). `npx tsc --noEmit` completed with zero errors, and `npx eslint src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingGenerationTasks.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx` completed with zero errors or hook-dependency warnings. Data-flow trace confirmed: registry builds `generationAssetMap` and `activeTaskAssetKeys` in `useActiveTaskClips` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:145-256`), `TimelineEditor` derives `isTaskActive` from that set and passes it to `ClipAction` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:218,411-462`), and `ClipAction` renders the spinner path with stale-badge fallback plus memo equality coverage (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372,406-448`). Realtime cleanup was verified by inspection of `useActiveTaskClips` returning the `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent(...)` unsubscribe from its effect (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:221-242`). A one-off script reproduced this integration contract, printed `Active task clip integration verified.`, and was deleted immediately after execution.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the matcher now covers all requested travel-task generation reference fields: root `input_image_generation_ids`, `start_image_generation_id`, `end_image_generation_id`, and nested `orchestrator_details.input_image_generation_ids`, matching the resolver payload fields persisted in `travelBetweenImages.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts`.

Yes. `useActiveTaskClips` performs a single project-wide active-task query (`lines 177-191`) and filters those results client-side against the registry-derived generation map (`lines 154-175`, `193-211`) rather than issuing per-clip queries. It also includes both realtime invalidation (`lines 221-242`) and optimistic clip state with exported `addOptimisticActive` plus 8-second timeout handling (`lines 24-123`).

Yes. The render path is now mutually exclusive: `ClipAction` shows `isTaskActive ? <spinner> : isVariantStale ? <stale badge> : null`, so a stale badge is not rendered when a task is active (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372`). `isTaskActive` is also included in `areClipActionPropsEqual` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:414`), so memoized clips update correctly when task activity changes.

Yes. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors, and the exact four-file ESLint command passed with zero errors, including no `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` findings. The final trace is intact: registry -> `useActiveTaskClips` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:145-256`) -> `activeTaskAssetKeys` lookup in `TimelineEditor` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:218,411-462`) -> `ClipAction` `isTaskActive` prop -> mutually exclusive spinner render (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372`). Realtime subscription cleanup remains correct because the effect returns the unsubscribe from `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent(...)` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:221-242`).
